steps.title: Extract the vertex from vertex form
steps.content: The equation $y = -4.9(x - 9.1)^2 + 10,700$ is already in vertex form $y = a(x - h)^2 + k$. Reading directly: the vertex is at $(9.1, 10,700)$. Since the leading coefficient is $-4.9$ (negative), the parabola opens downward, so the vertex represents the maximum point.

steps.title: Interpret the vertex in the context of the problem
steps.content: In this scenario, $x$ represents the number of seconds since the maneuver began, and $y$ represents height in meters. The vertex $(9.1, 10,700)$ tells us that 9.1 seconds after the maneuver started, the plane reached a maximum height of 10,700 meters. That matches choice B — B is correct.
